_comment: Claim-fidelity spec for the setup skill, GREENFIELD scenario: does a real `/charness:setup` run on a brand-new repo honor its own routing? setup branches on detected repo mode (SKILL.md steps 1, 78-83) and greenfield-flow vs normalization-flow are mutually exclusive, so this is the greenfield-branch fixture. CAPTURE CONTEXT (non-claim): this scenario is NOT capturable inside the mature charness repo — running `/charness:setup` here detects PARTIAL/NORMALIZE and never reaches greenfield-flow.md. A live capture requires running the slash command in a fresh/empty repo sandbox; until then this fixture is a static classification, not a captured run. requiredCommandFragments rationale: a greenfield run must consult greenfield-flow.md (minimum questions + expected surfaces, step 1 greenfield path), agent-docs-policy.md (host instruction surface created from scratch, step 2), and default-surfaces.md (the surface set scaffolded in step 4). thresholds omitted until a first baseline capture sets a runtime budget.
prompt: /charness:setup  bootstrap the initial operating surface for this brand-new empty repository from minimal ideation

agent-docs-policy.md.rationale: This reference owns the explicit repo-level host policy (AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md normalization) that step 2 requires for stabilizing the host-facing instruction surface first, including the both-missing create-and-symlink case a greenfield repo hits.

bootstrap-seams.md.rationale: This reference covers optional adapter, policy, or runtime seams that setup may seed only when the repo explicitly uses those Charness features.
bootstrap-seams.md.trigger: repo uses optional Charness seams beyond default surfaces (worktree, T-events, usage episodes, bounded review delegation, skill routing, artifact policy, announcement workflows).

default-surfaces.md.rationale: This reference defines all default operating surfaces (README, AGENTS, Roadmap, Operator Acceptance) that step 4 uses to scaffold the basic durable surfaces for a greenfield repo.

github-actions-defaults.md.rationale: This reference provides specific GitHub Actions version guidance only when the representative run detects a repo using GitHub-hosted workflows; repos without Actions do not consult it.
github-actions-defaults.md.trigger: repo scaffolds or normalizes GitHub-hosted workflows and needs current documented majors for first-party JavaScript actions.

greenfield-flow.md.rationale: In a greenfield representative run, this reference defines the minimum questions and expected surfaces that shape repo mode detection and initial surface scaffolding in step 1.

normalization-flow.md.rationale: In the greenfield scenario no prior durable surface exists to realign, so the normalization path is not taken; it is consulted only if mode detection reclassifies the repo as partially initialized.
normalization-flow.md.trigger: repo mode detection finds some existing surface (PARTIAL/NORMALIZE) rather than a greenfield repo.

operator-acceptance-synthesis.md.rationale: This reference describes a synthesis approach for operator-acceptance.md when the repo already has functional checks to derive from, not for the default template scaffolding a greenfield repo needs.
operator-acceptance-synthesis.md.trigger: mature repo already has real functional checks and wants to derive operator-acceptance.md from existing command and takeover surface instead of inventing a parallel ritual.

probe-surface.md.rationale: This narrow reference answers how to bootstrap a probe surface when the repo exposes an installable surface, which does not appear in every representative run.
probe-surface.md.trigger: repo ships an installable CLI, plugin, package, or agent-facing integration surface that needs a small explicit probe surface for install and health semantics.

retro-memory-seam.md.rationale: This reference provides opt-in seeding of retro adapter and lessons file only when the repo explicitly requests durable retro memory, not a default surface.
retro-memory-seam.md.trigger: repo wants durable retrospective pickup between sessions instead of chat-only retros.
